title: Near-Earth asteroids discovered over time
subtitle: Cumulative number of discovered asteroids with an orbit that brings them into proximity with Earth. This is broken down by size of the asteroid.
caption:
Near-Earth asteroids discovered over time

Cumulative number of discovered asteroids with an orbit that brings them into proximity with Earth. This is broken down by size of the asteroid.

The chart displays the cumulative count of Near-Earth asteroids discovered from 1990 to 2025, categorized by physical size. The vertical axis measures the number of asteroids from 0 to 40,000, while the horizontal axis spans the years. The data is presented as a stacked area chart with three distinct bands. The bottom band, colored dark grey, represents asteroids larger than 1 kilometer; this category remains relatively flat and low, staying well below the 2,000 mark throughout the entire period. The middle band, colored purple, indicates asteroids between 140 meters and 1 kilometer. This segment shows steady growth, rising from near zero in 1990 to approximately 10,000 by 2025. The top and largest band, colored orange, represents asteroids smaller than 140 meters. This category drives the overall upward trend, expanding rapidly especially after 2010. The total cumulative count, represented by the top edge of the orange area, climbs from near zero in 1990 to roughly 40,000 by 2025. The slope of the total curve becomes visibly steeper in the latter half of the timeline, indicating an acceleration in the rate of discovery. The note clarifies that Near-Earth asteroids are defined as those with a closest approach to the Sun of less than 1.3 astronomical units, where 1 AU equals the distance from Earth to the Sun.
